author: Marcus Daley
purpose: Design system refactor prompt for Claude to use cowork-skills, ui-ux-pro-max, and karpathy-skills when rebuilding VetAssist UI with military-authentic visual identity

load_before_executing.reason: Anti-slop enforcement — commits to a conceptual direction before any code is written
60-30-10 palette math, WCAG contrast ratios, 8px grid, type scale ratios

load_before_executing.install: npm install -g uipro-cli && uipro init --ai claude --global
load_before_executing.reason: 161 reasoning rules + 67 named styles + 57 font pairings — use as the design decision database
Think before coding, define success criteria first, surgical changes only
All component code must pass Marcus Daley's universal standards
The Grandparent Test — 75-year-old veteran, 48px touch targets, 18px default font, non-dismissable crisis banner

design_brief.product: VetAssist — Your AI Battle Buddy for VA Benefits
design_brief.audience: US veterans — broad age range, includes Vietnam-era (70s+), Gulf War, Post-9/11. Many deal with TBI, PTSD, chronic pain. Some are not tech-native.
design_brief.tone: Honored service. Quiet authority. This is not a startup. It is not a government portal. It is a battle buddy — trusted, direct, and completely on the veteran's side.
design_brief.problem_with_current_design: The prior UI output was AI slop — generic dark dashboard with no point of view, no connection to the veteran identity, no memorable visual anchor. Inter font, plain dark surface, looked like every other SaaS tool built in 2023. Veterans deserve better.
design_brief.design_mandate: Build a UI that feels like it was made BY a veteran FOR veterans. Incorporate real military visual language — the American flag, service imagery, the texture of worn gear, stars, stripes, the weight of service. Do it with design precision, not clip art energy.

aesthetic_direction.description: Military heritage editorial — the visual language of a high-quality veteran magazine or a well-funded veteran nonprofit, not a government form or a fintech dashboard
aesthetic_direction.conceptual_anchor: The American flag is not decoration. It is the emotional core. It appears as a textured background element — deeply muted, desaturated, present as atmosphere not advertisement. Stars become a subtle motif in the grid. The red stripe becomes the accent color. This is visual respect, not patriotic kitsch.

color_system.note: Derived from US flag + military olive — applied with 60-30-10 discipline

60_dominant.name: Field Black
60_dominant.usage: Base surface — near-black with a faint cool undertone, like the night sky over a base

30_secondary.name: Aged Canvas
30_secondary.usage: Card and panel surfaces — the color of old military canvas or worn denim

10_accent.name: Old Glory Red
10_accent.usage: Primary actions, CTAs, crisis banner, active state indicators — used sparingly and intentionally

text_primary.usage: Main body text — warm off-white, like aged paper

star_white.usage: High emphasis text, icon fills, star motifs

field_blue.usage: Union blue — hero section backgrounds, feature section dividers

stripe_red_muted.usage: Subtle stripe texture in backgrounds — never full-saturation

military_olive.usage: Success states, service branch badges, 'approved' indicators

score_gradient.note: Score ring — red (0-39%), gold (40-69%), field green (70-100%)

crisis_banner.note: Deep military red — non-negotiable, never changed

typography.note: Military precision meets editorial gravitas. No Inter. No Roboto. No Space Grotesk.

heading_font.reason: Oswald is condensed, strong, stencil-adjacent — reads like dog tags or a military stencil. Authoritative without being bureaucratic.

body_font.reason: Serif body reads like long-form journalism — the kind in a respected veterans' magazine. Warm, serious, readable at 18px. Veterans trust print-style typography.

mono_font.usage: Certificate IDs, file names, form field values, deletion certificate data

type_scale.note: Base is 18px NOT 16px — accessibility mandate from claude/skills/story_builder_decision_accessibility.md

flag_background.technique: Use the American flag as a CSS background-image or SVG texture at very low opacity (0.04–0.08) on hero sections and page backgrounds. It should be felt, not seen. Like a watermark of service.
flag_background.implementation: SVG flag stripes as repeating linear-gradient — alternating #7A1A1A and transparent at 4% opacity. Star field as scattered white dots at 3% opacity using radial-gradient or SVG pattern.
flag_background.wrong_way: Do NOT use a full-color flag photo. Do NOT make it bright or prominent. This is atmosphere, not a bumper sticker.

grain_overlay.technique: Subtle film grain or noise texture over dark surfaces using CSS filter or SVG feTurbulence at 2-4% opacity
grain_overlay.reason: Gives the dark surfaces a worn, physical quality — like aged military canvas or a matte tactical surface

veteran_imagery.usage: Hero sections and feature cards may use desaturated / duotone military imagery — silhouettes of veterans, hands shaking, a soldier's boots, a dog tag, a folded flag. NEVER stock-photo smiling people. NEVER combat/weapons.
veteran_imagery.treatment: Apply CSS duotone filter in Field Blue (#1A2744) + Old Glory Red (#B22234), or full desaturate with 60% opacity overlay of Field Black
veteran_imagery.implementation: img { filter: grayscale(80%) contrast(1.1); } with overlay div using mix-blend-mode: multiply

stripe_dividers.technique: Section dividers use a thin 3px horizontal line alternating between Old Glory Red (#B22234) and transparent — evoking the flag's stripes without being literal
stripe_dividers.placement: Between major page sections. Not between every card.

star_motif.technique: Five-pointed star (★) used sparingly as a bullet point replacement for key callouts, badge decorations, and section markers. Never more than 3 per screen.

spatial_composition.layout_style: Editorial grid with military precision — columns are clean but content is allowed to break the grid intentionally at hero level
spatial_composition.card_style: Sharp corners (4px radius only — not the rounded-2xl softness of consumer apps). Cards feel like mission briefing documents.
spatial_composition.borders: Thin 1px borders in #2A3149. Left-border accents in Old Glory Red for active/selected states.
spatial_composition.negative_space: Generous. Veterans with TBI need breathing room. Minimum 48px between major sections.
spatial_composition.asymmetry: Allowed in hero sections only. Content sections should be structurally disciplined.

motion.philosophy: Every animation must have a reason. Military precision — nothing moves without purpose.
allowed: ScoreRing fill — 800ms ease-out, single play on mount. The flagship animation.
Page entrance — 200ms fade + 8px translateY, staggered for card grids (50ms delay between cards)
Hover states — 150ms transition on border-color and background
Crisis banner pulse — slow 3s opacity pulse (0.85 to 1.0) to draw attention without causing distress
motion.prefers_reduced_motion: ALL animations must be wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). When reduced motion is set: instant transitions only.

component_refactor_targets.note: Rebuild each component from scratch using this design system. Check packages/ui-components/src/ for current state.

CrisisLineBanner.current_problem: Generic red bar with no visual weight or authority

refactor.icon: White phone icon (24px) + white star (★) left of text
refactor.text: Oswald 600 14px uppercase letter-spacing: 0.06em — 'VETERANS CRISIS LINE: CALL 988 PRESS 1 | TEXT 838255 | CHAT VETERANSCRISISLINE.NET'
refactor.behavior: Entire bar is a pressable link. Slow pulse animation (3s, opacity 0.9-1.0). Never dismissable.

ScoreRing.current_problem: Generic circular progress — looks like a fitness app

refactor.size: 140px diameter — large enough to feel authoritative
refactor.gradient: score_gradient from design system (red → gold → field green)

center_text.percentage: Oswald 700 32px — the number must feel like a military readout
center_text.label: Source Serif 4 12px #8A96AA — category name below percentage

refactor.tick_marks: 24 equally-spaced 1px tick marks around the outside ring at 20% opacity — like a precision instrument gauge
refactor.animation: Stroke-dasharray from 0 to value over 800ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)

DocumentDropZone.current_problem: Plain dashed box — looks like any file upload widget

refactor.icon: Document icon with a subtle star watermark behind it
refactor.sub_text: Source Serif 4 14px #8A96AA — 'PDF, JPG, PNG, HEIC, TIFF, DOCX, TXT — Max 25MB per file'

thumbnail_tray.thumbnail_card: 80x80px, sharp corners, 1px border #2A3149, label input below

PIIWarningModal.current_problem: Generic modal — could be any warning

refactor.header_row: Warning triangle icon (Old Glory Red) + star motif (★) + 'SENSITIVE INFORMATION DETECTED' in Oswald 600 16px uppercase
refactor.body_text: Source Serif 4 16px #EDF0F4 — 'We found [type] in your document and automatically covered it. Your information is protected.'
refactor.cta_button: Oswald 600 14px uppercase, Old Glory Red background, white text, sharp corners, full width

AIDisclosureBanner.current_problem: Forgettable info strip — veterans skip it

refactor.text: Source Serif 4 14px #8A96AA italic — 'VetAssist uses AI to provide educational information. This is not legal or medical advice. For claim-specific help, contact a VA-accredited VSO.'
refactor.dismiss: Small 'Got it' text button — collapses to 2px border line for session

NavSidebar.current_problem: Generic sidebar — looks like VS Code

logo_area.content: Star icon (★) + 'VETASSIST' in Oswald 700 16px uppercase + 'Your AI Battle Buddy' in Source Serif 4 11px #8A96AA

refactor.flag_watermark: American flag SVG at 3% opacity fixed at bottom of sidebar

HomeHero.current_problem: No hero exists yet — this sets the tone for everything

background.flag_texture: Horizontal stripe pattern — alternating #7A1A1A / transparent at 5% opacity
background.star_field: SVG scatter of 12-20 small 5-pointed stars at 4% opacity in upper-left quadrant (union position)

build.veteran_image: Desaturated silhouette photo (veteran with dog tags or folded flag) — right side, duotone Field Blue + slight vignette

headline.text: Your AI Battle Buddy for VA Benefits
headline.note: BATTLE BUDDY in Old Glory Red, rest in Star White

subheadline.text: Understand your benefits. Write better documents. Navigate the system.

build.stripe_divider: 3px horizontal line alternating #B22234 / transparent at bottom of hero

structure: Main content area (margin-left: 240px desktop, full-width mobile)
AIDisclosureBanner (on AI screens only, below nav)

success_criteria.note: Per karpathy-skills — define verifiable goals before building
pass: A veteran looking at the first screen immediately understands this was made FOR them, not FOR a startup pitch deck
The American flag is present as atmosphere in at least 3 screens — never as decoration, always as identity
Zero uses of Inter, Roboto, Arial, Space Grotesk, or system-ui
Zero purple, teal, coral, or standard SaaS accent colors
ScoreRing has tick marks and feels like a precision instrument, not a donut chart
CrisisLineBanner is visually the most prominent element on every screen
All text passes WCAG AA contrast minimum (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large)
Reduced motion is respected on all animations
Design could appear in a respected veteran-focused publication without looking out of place
fail: Any component could be copy-pasted into a generic SaaS product unchanged
The design looks like it was generated in 30 seconds with default prompts
Flag imagery is bright, patriotic-kitsch, or decorative
Any font from the do_not_use list appears anywhere
CrisisLineBanner is dismissable or visually de-emphasized

execution_instructions.step_1: Load all skills listed in skills_required.load_before_executing before writing any code
execution_instructions.step_2: Read docs/ui-design-spec.md for component behavior and interaction specifications
execution_instructions.step_3: For each component in component_refactor_targets: read the refactor spec, apply the design system values, build the component, validate against success_criteria
execution_instructions.step_4: Build components in packages/ui-components/src/ as shared TypeScript/React components — exported from index.ts
execution_instructions.step_5: After each component: run axe-core accessibility audit, verify WCAG contrast values, run verified-build-gate skill
execution_instructions.step_6: After all components complete: assemble HomeHero and page layout templates in apps/web/src/
execution_instructions.coding_standards: Every file needs the header comment: filename, developer (Marcus Daley), date, purpose. Single-line comments only (//). Zero hardcoded values — all colors/fonts as CSS variables from the design token file.
execution_instructions.design_token_file: Create packages/ui-components/src/tokens.css with all CSS custom properties from this prompt's color_system. All components import from this file — never hardcode hex values in component files.
